name: Islamic Scholarship Fund (ISF)
mission: The Islamic Scholarship Fund's (ISF) mission is to increase American Muslim representation in media and policy by providing access and funding through academic scholarships, internships, fellowships, film grants, and mentorship.
theoryOfChange: The Islamic Scholarship Fund (ISF) utilizes a 'Cycle of Support' as its programmatic model and strategy. This holistic approach supports American Muslim leaders throughout their academic and professional journeys, beginning with scholarships and mentorships, and continuing with internships, fellowships, career counseling, and continuing education. The organization's mission is to increase American Muslim representation in media and government to improve public policy and public opinion. This strategic framework aims to empower students and professionals to challenge harmful stereotypes and biases, and work towards a more equitable society. ISF's strategy involves education through scholarships, providing access through programs that bridge education and the professional workforce, and offering ongoing development and networking opportunities for alumni.
scoreSummary: Islamic Scholarship Fund (ISF) shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: All donations are tax-deductible to the fullest extent of the law, and ISF qualifies for Zakat. (Source: https://islamicscholarshipfund.org/donate/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 30 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 5.7% of revenue ($110,000 on $1,922,887)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

While its cost per beneficiary is calculated at $7,956.65, the organization maintains high data confidence and significant financial reserves, holding 30.2 months of working capital to ensure program longevity [1][5].

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking whether scholars actually enter their fields is the only way to prove the 'representation' mission is working.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="3">30.2 months of operating reserves</cite>, providing a massive buffer against economic volatility.
ISF demonstrates high efficacy in its mission, with <cite id="7">88% of scholars successfully entering their chosen professional fields</cite>.
The charity spends only <cite id="10">$0.17 to raise every $1</cite>, significantly outperforming many advocacy-sector peers.

strengths_deep_dive: ISF's strength lies in its targeted 'capacity building' model, which focuses on long-term systemic change rather than immediate relief. By securing <cite id="11">20 out of 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards</cite>, the organization proves its commitment to rigorous governance. Furthermore, its <cite id="12">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> reflects a high level of public disclosure regarding its <cite id="13">13 tracked outcome metrics</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Scholarship Fund (ISF) is a specialized advocacy and education organization <cite id="1">founded in 2009</cite> to address the underrepresentation of American Muslims in influential sectors such as law, media, and government. By providing strategic financial support and career pipelines, ISF seeks to empower a new generation of leaders to <cite id="2">shape public policy and narrative</cite>. The organization operates with a high degree of financial stability, maintaining <cite id="3">30.2 months of working capital</cite> to ensure the longevity of its fellowship and scholarship programs. In the 2023 fiscal year, ISF managed <cite id="4">total revenue of $1,014,133</cite>, utilizing these funds to support a diverse array of initiatives including <cite id="5">Congressional Policy Fellowships, Judicial Internships, and Film Grants</cite>. The organization’s impact is evidenced by its alumni success, with a <cite id="6">100% graduation rate</cite> among its scholars and <cite id="7">88% of recipients working in their field of study</cite>. ISF has reached a significant milestone in its history, having provided a <cite id="8">total of $3,060,000 in awards</cite> since its inception. While its program expense ratio of <cite id="9">65.0%</cite> demonstrates a strong commitment to direct service, the organization maintains an efficient administrative profile with <cite id="10">fundraising efficiency of $0.09 per $1 raised</cite>.
